headline: Why the second round of France's municipal elections matters
description: The vote on Sunday, March 22, will provide valuable national insights, with a focus on the far-right's result, the impact of mergers or divisions within the left and right and the right's gamble to stay in the race.

headline: Iranian missiles sow panic in Israeli town
Trump settles scores with former prosecutor Robert Mueller following news of his death
Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
description: Cuba's national power network failed for the second time in less than a week, leaving streets pitch black and forcing people to navigate using flashlights. Blackouts have intensified because of aging infrastructure and a US oil blockade.

headline: The UAE reorganizes its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
description: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.

headline: London exhibit highlights a meeting between two major suffragettes
description: The Florence Nightingale Museum, named after the nurse who elevated her profession and founded the first school dedicated to nursing, is hosting an exhibition devoted to the famous suffragette Emmeline Pankhurst.

headline: Michael Rider, Celine designer: 'Bringing joy and humor to fashion doesn't mean making funny clothes'
description: Since taking over as creative director of Celine in October 2024, the American designer, who succeeded Phoebe Philo and Hedi Slimane, has seized upon the brand's core principles, infusing them with his own positive and personal touch.

With conviction of jihadist Sabri Essid, French judiciary recognizes Yazidi genocide for first time
description: By sentencing Essid to life imprisonment for 'genocide' on Friday, the Paris court followed the example set by Germany, Sweden and Belgium. The Islamic State’s plan to 'destroy' this minority, which has never been the subject of an international investigation, is now recognized by several European courts.

headline: Catherine MacGregor, Engie CEO, on gas prices: 'There are many differences compared to 2022'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the head of the French energy group discusses the repercussions of the war in the Middle East. She believes that, thanks to nuclear and renewable energy, France is less exposed than other countries.
byline: Fabrice Gliszczynski,  Adrien Pécout and  Marie de Vergès

headline: An ultra-modern artist: 'Renoir and Love' and 'Renoir Drawings' at the Musée d'Orsay
description: Challenging his reputation as a sentimental artist, two exhibitions of paintings and drawings highlight Renoir's affinity for working-class subjects and the complexity of his compositions.
